# Warm-Up & Cool-Down Protocols

---

## 1. Strength: Upper Body Warm-Up (~10 min)

| Order | Exercise | Duration/Reps | Time | Cue |
|-------|----------|---------------|------|-----|
| 1 | Jumping jacks | 30 sec | 0:30 | Light and rhythmic, get blood flowing |
| 2 | Arm circles (forward + backward) | 15 sec each direction | 1:00 | Full range, gradually increase circle size |
| 3 | Shoulder CARs | 5 each direction per arm | 2:00 | Slow, controlled, maximal range — fist tight, irradiate tension |
| 4 | Band pull-aparts | 2 x 15 | 1:30 | Squeeze shoulder blades together, arms straight, chest proud |
| 5 | Push-up walkouts (inchworms) | 5 reps | 1:30 | Slow walk out to full plank, brief hold, walk hands back |
| 6 | Thoracic rotations (quadruped) | 5 each side | 1:30 | Hand behind head, rotate to open chest to ceiling, exhale at top |
| 7 | Light ramp-up set 1 (main lift) | 8 reps @ empty bar / very light | 1:00 | Focus on bar path and setup — no grinding |
| 8 | Light ramp-up set 2 (main lift) | 5 reps @ ~50% working weight | 1:00 | Increase speed slightly, lock in cues |

**Total: ~10 min**

---

## 2. Strength: Lower Body Warm-Up (~12 min)

| Order | Exercise | Duration/Reps | Time | Cue |
|-------|----------|---------------|------|-----|
| 1 | Light jog in place / high knees | 30 sec | 0:30 | Easy pace, wake up the legs |
| 2 | Hip CARs (standing) | 5 each direction per leg | 2:00 | Slow circles, keep pelvis stable, maximize range |
| 3 | 90/90 switches | 8 total (4 each side) | 1:30 | Knees at 90 degrees, sit tall, rotate hips smoothly |
| 4 | Ankle mobility (knee-over-toe rocks) | 10 each side | 1:00 | Heel stays down, drive knee past toes over 2nd/3rd toe |
| 5 | Bodyweight squats | 10 reps | 1:00 | Full depth, chest up, drive knees out over toes |
| 6 | Walking lunges | 8 each leg | 1:30 | Long stride, upright torso, back knee grazes floor |
| 7 | Banded lateral walks | 10 each direction | 1:00 | Band above knees, stay low in half squat, push knees out |
| 8 | Fire hydrants | 10 each side | 1:00 | Quadruped position, keep core braced, lift knee out to side |
| 9 | Light ramp-up set 1 (main lift) | 8 reps @ empty bar | 1:00 | Groove the pattern — focus on brace and depth |
| 10 | Light ramp-up set 2 (main lift) | 5 reps @ ~50% working weight | 1:00 | Controlled descent, strong drive out of the hole |

**Total: ~12 min**

---

## 3. Strength: Power Day Warm-Up (~11 min)

| Order | Exercise | Duration/Reps | Time | Cue |
|-------|----------|---------------|------|-----|
| 1 | Jumping jacks | 30 sec | 0:30 | Brisk pace, full arm extension overhead |
| 2 | High knees | 30 sec | 0:30 | Drive knees to hip height, pump arms |
| 3 | Leg swings (front-to-back) | 10 each leg | 1:00 | Hold wall for balance, keep standing leg straight |
| 4 | Leg swings (lateral) | 10 each leg | 1:00 | Cross body and out, controlled swing |
| 5 | Arm circles + shoulder pass-throughs | 10 each + 10 pass-throughs | 1:00 | Use PVC or band for pass-throughs — wide grip, go slow |
| 6 | Hip CARs | 3 each direction per leg | 1:30 | Smaller set here — just open up the hips |
| 7 | Hang clean technique (empty bar) | 2 x 5 reps | 2:00 | Focus on hip hinge, explosive hip extension, high elbows in catch |
| 8 | Box jump build-up (low box) | 3 reps to low box | 1:00 | Soft landing, full hip extension on takeoff, step down |
| 9 | Box jump build-up (working height) | 3 reps | 1:00 | Stick the landing, quiet feet, absorb through hips |
| 10 | Light ramp-up set (main power lift) | 3 reps @ ~50% | 1:00 | Fast and crisp — prime the nervous system |

**Total: ~11 min**

---

## 4. MMA Striking Warm-Up (~10 min)

| Order | Exercise | Duration/Reps | Time | Cue |
|-------|----------|---------------|------|-----|
| 1 | Jump rope | 3 min | 3:00 | Stay on balls of feet, light bounce, relaxed shoulders |
| 2 | Wrist circles | 15 each direction | 0:30 | Interlace fingers, roll smoothly — protect the wrists |
| 3 | Neck warm-up (nods, rotations, lateral) | 10 each direction | 1:00 | Slow and controlled — never force range, gentle end-range holds |
| 4 | Arm circles + shoulder rolls | 10 each direction | 0:30 | Loosen up the striking chain from shoulder to fist |
| 5 | Dynamic hip openers (leg swings, hip circles) | 8 each leg | 1:00 | Prepare for kicks — front-to-back and lateral swings |
| 6 | Torso rotations (standing) | 10 each side | 0:30 | Feet planted, rotate from thoracic spine, arms relaxed |
| 7 | Shadow boxing — jab-cross only | 1 min | 1:00 | Light and technical, focus on hand return and stance |
| 8 | Shadow boxing — add hooks, uppercuts | 1 min | 1:00 | Stay relaxed, snap punches, keep chin tucked |
| 9 | Shadow boxing — full combos + kicks | 1 min | 1:00 | Put it all together, progressive intensity, move feet |

**Total: ~10 min**

---

## 5. MMA Grappling Warm-Up (~12 min)

| Order | Exercise | Duration/Reps | Time | Cue |
|-------|----------|---------------|------|-----|
| 1 | Light jogging / movement on mats | 3 min | 3:00 | Include shuffles, backpedals, sprawl checks — keep it easy |
| 2 | Neck warm-up (nods, rotations, bridges) | 10 each direction | 1:00 | Gentle — build up bridge height gradually, never force |
| 3 | Hip escapes / shrimps | 5 each side (down and back) | 1:30 | Drive off the bottom foot, create space, keep elbows tight |
| 4 | Technical stand-ups | 5 each side | 1:30 | Post on hand, kick leg through, stand to fighting stance |
| 5 | Granby rolls | 5 each direction | 1:30 | Tuck chin, roll over shoulder, stay compact |
| 6 | Partner pummeling (if available) | 2 min | 2:00 | Fight for underhooks, stay square, heavy hips |
| 7 | Partner arm drags (if available) | 5 each side | 1:30 | Grip wrist and tricep, pull across body, angle off |

*Solo alternative for exercises 6-7: replace with forward/backward rolls (5 each) and shot entries (5 each side).*

**Total: ~12 min**

---

## 6. Conditioning / Cardio Warm-Up (~8 min)

| Order | Exercise | Duration/Reps | Time | Cue |
|-------|----------|---------------|------|-----|
| 1 | Light movement (walk / easy jog / pedal) | 2 min | 2:00 | Zone 1 effort — conversational pace, loosen up |
| 2 | Leg swings (front-to-back + lateral) | 8 each direction, each leg | 1:30 | Controlled, progressively bigger swings |
| 3 | Bodyweight squats | 10 reps | 0:30 | Full depth, open up hips and ankles |
| 4 | Torso rotations + arm swings | 10 each | 0:30 | Wake up the trunk, stay relaxed |
| 5 | Progressive build-up on modality — easy | 1 min @ ~50% target intensity | 1:00 | Find your rhythm, smooth technique |
| 6 | Progressive build-up — moderate | 1 min @ ~70% target intensity | 1:00 | HR climbing, breathing picks up but controlled |
| 7 | Progressive build-up — near target | 1 min @ ~85-90% target intensity | 1:00 | Approaching working pace, mentally lock in |

**Total: ~8 min**

---

## 7. Cool-Down Protocol (Universal — After Every Session, ~15-20 min)

### Phase 1: Light Movement (5 min)

| Exercise | Duration | Cue |
|----------|----------|-----|
| Walking (or easy bike / treadmill) | 3 min | Slow pace, bring heart rate down gradually |
| Deep breathing (standing or seated) | 2 min | 4-count inhale through nose, 6-count exhale through mouth |

### Phase 2: Static Stretching (5 min — 30 sec holds each)

| Order | Stretch | Duration | Cue |
|-------|---------|----------|-----|
| 1 | Standing hamstring stretch (each leg) | 30 sec x 2 | Hinge at hips, flat back, feel stretch behind knee |
| 2 | Kneeling hip flexor stretch (each side) | 30 sec x 2 | Squeeze glute on back leg, tall torso, push hips forward |
| 3 | Standing quad stretch (each leg) | 30 sec x 2 | Pull heel to glute, knees together, stand tall |
| 4 | Doorway / post chest stretch (each side) | 30 sec x 2 | Arm at 90 degrees on wall, lean through, open chest |
| 5 | Overhead lat stretch (each side) | 30 sec x 2 | Grab overhead surface, sink hips away, feel side/back stretch |

### Phase 3: Foam Rolling (5-10 min)

| Priority Area | Duration | Cue |
|---------------|----------|-----|
| Quads / hip flexors | 60-90 sec each leg | Slow rolls, pause on tender spots 10-15 sec |
| IT band / outer thigh | 60 sec each leg | Roll from hip to knee, go slow — this one is intense |
| Upper back (thoracic) | 60-90 sec | Arms crossed over chest, roll mid-back, extend over roller |
| Lats | 60 sec each side | Side-lying, roller in armpit area, roll down to mid-rib |
| Glutes (lacrosse ball optional) | 60 sec each side | Sit on roller/ball, cross ankle over knee, target deep glute |
| Calves | 60 sec each leg | Stack legs for pressure, point/flex foot while rolling |

**Total: ~15-20 min**

---

## 8. Mobility Flow (Recovery Days / Week 4 Wednesday PM, ~25 min)

*Perform as a continuous flow. Breathe deeply throughout — exhale into each stretch. No rushing.*

| Order | Position / Exercise | Duration | Cue |
|-------|---------------------|----------|-----|
| 1 | Cat-cow (quadruped) | 90 sec (slow reps) | Inhale into extension, exhale into flexion — full spinal wave |
| 2 | Thread the needle (each side) | 60 sec per side | Reach under and through, follow hand with eyes, feel thoracic rotation |
| 3 | Open books (side-lying, each side) | 60 sec per side | Knees stacked, top arm opens to ceiling, let gravity pull you open |
| 4 | 90/90 hip stretch — front leg focus (each side) | 90 sec per side | Both knees at 90, sit tall, hinge forward over front shin |
| 5 | 90/90 hip stretch — back leg focus (each side) | 60 sec per side | Same setup, lean back slightly, feel inner thigh / hip of back leg |
| 6 | Pigeon pose (each side) | 90 sec per side | Front shin angled, back leg extended, walk hands forward to deepen |
| 7 | Frog stretch | 90 sec | Knees wide, hips push back, forearms on floor — go easy at first |
| 8 | Shoulder CARs (standing) | 60 sec (3 each direction per arm) | Slow and maximal — own every degree of the circle |
| 9 | Sleeper stretch (each side) | 60 sec per side | Side-lying, bottom arm at 90, gently press hand toward floor for internal rotation |
| 10 | Supine hamstring stretch (each leg) | 60 sec per side | Use strap or towel, keep leg straight, pull gently toward face |
| 11 | Ankle mobility (half-kneeling, each side) | 60 sec per side | Drive knee past toes, heel stays down, feel stretch in calf/ankle |
| 12 | Wrist mobility (quadruped) | 60 sec | Fingers forward, lean in — then fingers back, lean gently — then backs of hands |
| 13 | Seated spinal twist (each side) | 60 sec per side | Cross leg over, twist toward bent knee, press elbow outside knee |
| 14 | Child's pose | 90 sec | Arms extended, sink hips to heels, breathe into lower back |

**Total: ~25 min**

---

### Quick Reference: Warm-Up Assignment by Session

| Session Type | Warm-Up Protocol | Time |
|--------------|------------------|------|
| Strength: Upper Body | Protocol 1 | ~10 min |
| Strength: Lower Body | Protocol 2 | ~12 min |
| Strength: Power Day | Protocol 3 | ~11 min |
| MMA Striking | Protocol 4 | ~10 min |
| MMA Grappling | Protocol 5 | ~12 min |
| Conditioning / Cardio | Protocol 6 | ~8 min |
| All sessions (post) | Cool-Down Protocol 7 | ~15-20 min |
| Recovery day | Mobility Flow Protocol 8 | ~25 min |

# MMA Nutrition Framework

**Athlete Profile:** 87-90 kg (88 kg reference weight) | Amateur MMA Fighter

---

## 1. Macro Calculations

**Core Principle:** Protein stays constant every day. Carbs cycle based on training demand. Fat stays relatively stable.

### Per-Kilogram Targets (88 kg)

| Nutrient | g/kg     | Grams | Notes                        |
|----------|----------|-------|------------------------------|
| Protein  | 2.1      | 185 g | Constant across all days     |
| Carbs    | Varies   | —     | Cycled by training volume    |
| Fat      | ~1.0     | 85-88 g | Minimum for hormonal health |

### Daily Totals by Day Type

| Day Type                  | Protein | Carbs | Fat  | kcal    | Carb g/kg |
|---------------------------|---------|-------|------|---------|-----------|
| **Training Day**          | 185 g   | 400 g | 85 g | ~3,100  | 4.5       |
| **High-Volume Double Day**| 185 g   | 440 g | 88 g | ~3,300* | 5.0       |
| **Rest Day**              | 185 g   | 264 g | 85 g | ~2,560  | 3.0       |

*\*Double session days: base intake is ~3,300 kcal + intra-workout carbs bring effective total to ~3,600 kcal.*

### The Math

```
Protein:  88 kg × 2.1 g/kg = 185 g → 185 × 4 =  740 kcal
Fat:      88 kg × 1.0 g/kg =  85 g →  85 × 9 =  765 kcal

Training day carbs:    88 × 4.5 = 400 g → 400 × 4 = 1,600 kcal → Total: 3,105 kcal
Double day carbs:      88 × 5.0 = 440 g → 440 × 4 = 1,760 kcal → Total: 3,265 kcal + intra
Rest day carbs:        88 × 3.0 = 264 g → 264 × 4 = 1,056 kcal → Total: 2,561 kcal
```

---

## 2. Meal Timing

### Pre-Training (2-3 hours before)

| Nutrient | Target     | Grams (88 kg) | Goal                              |
|----------|------------|----------------|------------------------------------|
| Carbs    | 1-2 g/kg   | 88-176 g       | Top off glycogen                   |
| Protein  | 0.3-0.4 g/kg | 26-35 g      | Start muscle protein synthesis     |
| Fat      | Low        | < 15 g         | Speed gastric emptying             |

### Pre-Training Snack (30-60 min before)

- **30-50 g fast-digesting carbs** — banana, white rice cake with honey, sports drink
- Keep fat and fiber minimal to avoid GI distress during training

### Intra-Workout (double sessions or any session > 90 min)

- **30-60 g carbs per hour** — sports drink, gummy bears, highly branched cyclic dextrin
- **Electrolytes** — 500-700 mg sodium per liter of fluid
- Sip continuously; do not chug between rounds

### Post-Training (within 1-2 hours)

| Nutrient | Target       | Grams (88 kg) | Goal                          |
|----------|--------------|----------------|-------------------------------|
| Protein  | 0.3-0.5 g/kg | 26-44 g        | Kickstart recovery/MPS        |
| Carbs    | 1-1.5 g/kg   | 88-132 g       | Replenish glycogen             |

---

## 3. Sample Meal Plans

### Training Day (~3,100 kcal)

| Meal | Time | Food | P | C | F | kcal |
|------|------|------|---|---|---|------|
| **Meal 1 — Breakfast** | 7:00 AM | 4 whole eggs scrambled, 2 slices sourdough toast, 1 banana, 1 tbsp butter | 32 g | 65 g | 28 g | 640 |
| **Meal 2 — Pre-Training** | 11:00 AM | 150 g chicken breast, 250 g white rice (cooked), mixed greens salad, 1 tbsp olive oil | 45 g | 90 g | 18 g | 700 |
| **Pre-Training Snack** | 12:30 PM | 1 banana + 1 tbsp honey | 1 g | 42 g | 0 g | 170 |
| **Intra-Training** | 1:00-3:00 PM | 500 ml sports drink + 30 g carb powder | 0 g | 55 g | 0 g | 220 |
| **Meal 3 — Post-Training** | 3:30 PM | 40 g whey protein, 100 g oats (dry weight), 200 g blueberries, 1 tbsp peanut butter | 45 g | 100 g | 14 g | 710 |
| **Meal 4 — Dinner** | 7:00 PM | 180 g salmon fillet, 200 g sweet potato, 150 g broccoli, 1 tsp olive oil | 42 g | 50 g | 20 g | 550 |
| **Meal 5 — Pre-Bed** | 9:30 PM | 200 g Greek yogurt (full fat), 30 g walnuts, 30 g dark chocolate | 20 g | 25 g | 22 g | 380 |
| **DAILY TOTAL** | | | **185 g** | **427 g** | **102 g** | **~3,370** |

*Adjust portions down slightly on standard training days (reduce rice at Meal 2 or oats at Meal 3 by ~25 g dry) to hit 3,100. The plan above is closer to a moderate-heavy session day. On double days, keep full portions and add a second intra-workout drink.*

### Rest Day (~2,560 kcal)

| Meal | Time | Food | P | C | F | kcal |
|------|------|------|---|---|---|------|
| **Meal 1 — Breakfast** | 8:30 AM | 3 whole eggs + 2 egg whites scrambled, 1 slice sourdough, 1/2 avocado, 100 g cherry tomatoes | 35 g | 30 g | 24 g | 480 |
| **Meal 2 — Lunch** | 12:30 PM | 180 g lean ground beef (93%), 150 g white rice (cooked), roasted vegetables (zucchini, peppers), 1 tsp olive oil | 45 g | 55 g | 18 g | 560 |
| **Meal 3 — Afternoon** | 4:00 PM | 30 g whey protein, 1 apple, 25 g almonds | 30 g | 35 g | 14 g | 390 |
| **Meal 4 — Dinner** | 7:00 PM | 150 g chicken thigh (skin removed), 200 g roasted potatoes, large mixed salad, 1 tbsp olive oil dressing | 40 g | 50 g | 20 g | 540 |
| **Meal 5 — Pre-Bed** | 9:30 PM | 250 g cottage cheese, 15 g honey, 20 g pumpkin seeds | 35 g | 25 g | 10 g | 330 |
| **DAILY TOTAL** | | | **185 g** | **195 g** | **86 g** | **~2,300** |

*If coming in under the 264 g carb target, add a piece of fruit at Meal 2 and an extra 50 g rice at lunch. Adjust carbs upward with easy additions (bread, rice, fruit) rather than adding fat.*

---

## 4. Hydration Protocol

### Daily Baseline

| Metric | Calculation | Target |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| Body weight method | 88 kg × 35-45 ml/kg | **3.1 - 4.0 L per day** |

Aim for the higher end on training days and in warm conditions.

### Around Training

| Window | Protocol |
|--------|----------|
| **Pre-training** (2 hours before) | 500 ml water, steadily sipped |
| **During training** | 600-1,200 ml per hour with electrolytes |
| **Post-training** | Replace 150% of fluid lost (weigh before/after if possible) |

### Electrolyte Targets During Training

- **Sodium:** 500-700 mg per liter of fluid
- **Potassium:** 150-200 mg per liter
- Add a pinch of salt to water or use a commercial electrolyte mix

### Monitoring

- **Urine color:** Aim for pale straw yellow. Clear = overhydrated. Dark yellow/amber = dehydrated.
- Check first thing in the morning — this is your best baseline indicator.
- If weigh-in shows > 2% body weight loss post-session, increase fluid intake next session.

---

## 5. Supplement Stack

| Supplement | Dose | Timing | Evidence | Key Benefit | Notes |
|------------|------|--------|----------|-------------|-------|
| **Creatine Monohydrate** | 5 g/day | Any time (consistency matters) | A-tier | Strength, power output, recovery | No loading phase needed. Take daily including rest days. Mix in water or shake. |
| **Caffeine** | 3-6 mg/kg (264-528 mg) | 45-60 min pre-training | A-tier | Alertness, power, endurance | **Avoid after 2:00 PM** to protect sleep. Start at lower dose, assess tolerance. Cycle off 1 week/month if desired. |
| **Whey Protein** | 30-40 g per serving | Post-training or any meal gap | A-tier | Convenient complete protein | Use to hit daily 185 g target. Not a replacement for whole food protein. |
| **Electrolytes** | 500-700 mg sodium/L | During training > 60 min | A-tier | Prevent cramping, maintain output | Commercial mix or DIY (1/4 tsp salt + water + squeeze of lemon). |
| **Vitamin D3** | 3,000-5,000 IU/day | With a fat-containing meal | A-tier | Immune function, bone health, recovery | Get blood levels tested; target 40-60 ng/ml. Higher dose in winter / low sun. |
| **Magnesium Glycinate** | 400-500 mg/day | Split AM/PM; prioritize before bed | B-tier | Sleep quality, muscle relaxation, recovery | Glycinate form is best absorbed and least likely to cause GI issues. |
| **Omega-3 (EPA/DHA)** | 2-3 g combined EPA+DHA/day | With meals (split across 2 meals) | A-tier | Anti-inflammatory, joint health, brain function | Look for high-concentration fish oil. Keep refrigerated. |
| **Beta-Alanine** | 3-6 g/day | Split into 2-3 doses (reduces tingling) | B-tier | Buffers lactic acid in 1-4 min efforts | Optional. Most useful for high-rep grappling rounds. Tingling (paresthesia) is harmless. |

---

## 6. Grocery List

- Chicken breast (boneless, skinless)

### Pantry Staples

## 7. Quick-Reference Daily Checklists

### Training Day

- [ ] Hit protein target: 185 g across all meals
- [ ] Hit carb target: ~400 g (scale up to 440 g on double days)
- [ ] Pre-training meal 2-3 hours before: carbs + protein, low fat

# Conditioning & Fitness Testing Protocol

## Week 0 — Baseline Testing Protocol

### When to Test

- Schedule testing **2–3 days before Week 1 begins** (e.g., if Week 1 starts Monday, test on the prior Thursday or Friday).
- Avoid testing the day before training starts — allow at least one full rest day between testing and Week 1 Day 1.
- If splitting across two days, do cardiovascular + muscular endurance on Day 1, strength + MMA-specific on Day 2.
- Record the exact date, time of day, and conditions (fasted/fed, sleep quality, etc.) so you can replicate them at re-test.

### Warm-Up Protocol

Perform this warm-up before each testing session (approximately 10 minutes):

1. **5 min easy cardio** — row, bike, or jog at conversational pace
2. **Joint circles** — ankles, knees, hips, shoulders, wrists (10 each direction)
3. **Dynamic stretches** — leg swings (10/side), hip circles (10/side), arm circles (10/side), inchworms (5)
4. **Movement prep** — 5 bodyweight squats, 5 push-ups, 5 pull-apart band pulls

For strength tests, add 2–3 progressive warm-up sets before working sets (e.g., empty bar → 50% → 70% → 85% → 3RM attempt).

### Order of Tests

Run tests from **least fatiguing to most fatiguing** to avoid one test skewing the next. The recommended order below groups tests logically and minimizes interference.

**Rest between tests:** 3–5 minutes between tests within a category, 5–10 minutes between categories.

---

### Test Battery

#### Cardiovascular

| # | Test | Protocol | What It Measures | Target / Benchmark |
|---|------|----------|------------------|--------------------|
| 1 | Resting Heart Rate | Measure on waking (before standing), 3 consecutive mornings, record average | Aerobic base / cardiac efficiency | < 60 bpm (well-trained); < 50 bpm (elite) |
| 2 | 2 km Row or Run | Time trial — all-out effort. Record finish time, HR at finish, and HR 1 min post-finish | Aerobic capacity (VO2max proxy) | Row: < 7:30 (good), < 7:00 (excellent). Run: < 8:30 (good), < 8:00 (excellent) |
| 3 | 5-min Assault Bike | Max calories in 5 minutes. Record total cals and average HR | Anaerobic capacity + sustained power | > 75 cal (good), > 90 cal (excellent) |

#### Strength

| # | Test | Protocol | What It Measures | Target / Benchmark |
|---|------|----------|------------------|--------------------|
| 4 | Est. 1RM Back Squat | Warm up progressively, work up to a heavy 3RM. Calculate 1RM using Epley formula | Lower body maximal strength | 1.5× bodyweight (good), 2× (excellent) |
| 5 | Est. 1RM Bench Press | Warm up progressively, work up to a heavy 3RM. Calculate 1RM using Epley formula | Upper body pressing strength | 1× bodyweight (good), 1.25× (excellent) |
| 6 | Est. 1RM Deadlift | Warm up progressively, work up to a heavy 3RM. Calculate 1RM using Epley formula | Posterior chain maximal strength | 2× bodyweight (good), 2.5× (excellent) |
| 7 | Max Strict Pull-Ups | Dead hang start, chin over bar, full extension at bottom. No kipping or swinging. Count total reps until failure | Upper body pulling strength-endurance | 10+ (good), 15+ (excellent), 20+ (elite) |

#### Muscular Endurance

| # | Test | Protocol | What It Measures | Target / Benchmark |
|---|------|----------|------------------|--------------------|
| 8 | Max Push-Ups in 2 min | Continuous — chest must touch floor, full lockout at top. Pausing in the up position is allowed. Stop at 2:00 | Upper body endurance | 50+ (good), 70+ (excellent) |
| 9 | Max Plank Hold | Forearm plank, elbows under shoulders, body in straight line. Time stops when hips sag or rise > 2 inches | Core endurance / anti-extension | 2:00+ (good), 3:00+ (excellent) |

#### MMA-Specific

| # | Test | Protocol | What It Measures | Target / Benchmark |
|---|------|----------|------------------|--------------------|
| 10 | 3-min Sprawl Test | Set a 3-minute timer. Perform max sprawls (standing → chest to floor → return to stance). Count total reps | Takedown defense endurance / anaerobic capacity | 30+ (good), 40+ (excellent) |
| 11 | Ground-to-Standing Transitions | Set a 2-minute timer. Start standing → lie flat on back → perform a technical stand-up → repeat. Count total reps | Scramble ability / MMA-specific endurance | 15+ (good), 20+ (excellent) |

#### Body Composition

| # | Test | Protocol | What It Measures | Target / Benchmark |
|---|------|----------|------------------|--------------------|
| 12 | Bodyweight | Fasted, morning, after using the bathroom. Same scale each time | Body mass | Track change, not absolute |
| 13 | Waist Circumference | Measure at navel level, relaxed (not sucking in). Use a cloth tape measure | Central body composition proxy | Track change, not absolute |

---

### Recording Template

| # | Test | Week 0 Result | Week 5 Re-Test | Change | Notes |
|---|------|---------------|----------------|--------|-------|
| 1 | Resting Heart Rate (bpm) | | | | 3-day avg |
| 2 | 2 km Row/Run (time) | | | | HR at finish: ___ / 1-min recovery HR: ___ |
| 3 | 5-min Assault Bike (cals) | | | | Avg HR: ___ |
| 4 | Back Squat 3RM → Est. 1RM | | | | 3RM weight: ___ |
| 5 | Bench Press 3RM → Est. 1RM | | | | 3RM weight: ___ |
| 6 | Deadlift 3RM → Est. 1RM | | | | 3RM weight: ___ |
| 7 | Max Strict Pull-Ups | | | | |
| 8 | Max Push-Ups in 2 min | | | | |
| 9 | Max Plank Hold (time) | | | | Reason for stopping: ___ |
| 10 | 3-min Sprawl Test (reps) | | | | |
| 11 | Ground-to-Standing (reps) | | | | |
| 12 | Bodyweight (kg/lb) | | | | Fasted, morning |
| 13 | Waist Circumference (cm/in) | | | | At navel |

---

## Week 5 — Re-Testing Protocol

### Scheduling

- Re-test on the **Saturday of Week 4** (the deload week).
- By this point you will have had 3–4 days of reduced training volume, so you should be fresh enough for maximal effort.
- Use the **exact same conditions** as Week 0: same time of day, same equipment, same warm-up, same test order, fasted or fed the same way.

### Procedure

1. Perform the same warm-up protocol as Week 0.
2. Run all 13 tests in the same order.
3. Record results in the "Week 5 Re-Test" column of the recording template above.
4. Calculate the "Change" column (absolute and percentage).

### 1RM Calculation — Epley Formula

Use the same formula for both Week 0 and Week 5 to ensure apples-to-apples comparison:

```
Estimated 1RM = Weight × (1 + Reps ÷ 30)
```

**Examples:**
- 3RM of 100 kg → 1RM = 100 × (1 + 3/30) = 100 × 1.10 = **110 kg**
- 3RM of 80 kg → 1RM = 80 × (1 + 3/30) = 80 × 1.10 = **88 kg**

Note: The Epley formula is most accurate for rep ranges of 1–10. Always use a 3RM for consistency.

### Expected Improvements After 4 Weeks

Realistic improvements for a motivated trainee following the program consistently:

| Category | Expected Change | Notes |
|----------|----------------|-------|
| Resting Heart Rate | −2 to −5 bpm | Aerobic base adaptations take time; modest change expected |
| 2 km Row/Run | −10 to −30 sec | Cardiac output and pacing improve |
| 5-min Assault Bike | +3 to +8 cal | Anaerobic threshold improvement |
| Squat / Bench / Deadlift 1RM | +3–8% | Neuromuscular adaptations dominate in early weeks |
| Max Pull-Ups | +1 to +4 reps | Strength-endurance gains |
| Push-Ups in 2 min | +5 to +10 reps | Muscular endurance adapts quickly |
| Plank Hold | +15 to +30 sec | Core endurance responds well to consistent training |
| 3-min Sprawl Test | +5 to +10 reps | Improved anaerobic capacity + movement efficiency |
| Ground-to-Standing | +3 to +5 reps | Better technique + conditioning |
| Bodyweight | −1 to −3 kg (if in deficit) or stable | Depends on nutrition targets |
| Waist Circumference | −1 to −3 cm | Body composition recomposition |

### Interpreting Results

- **Significant improvement (> 10%):** The program is working well for this quality. Maintain or progress.
- **Modest improvement (3–10%):** Normal for 4 weeks. Keep training consistently.
- **No change or regression:** Check recovery, nutrition, sleep, and stress. Consider whether this area needs more volume or different stimulus in the next block.
- **Strength up + endurance down (or vice versa):** May indicate interference effect — review concurrent training balance.

### Next Steps After Re-Test

- Use Week 5 results as the new baseline for the next 4-week block.
- Adjust training loads: new 3RM estimates should inform working weights.
- Identify weakest areas and consider prioritizing them in the next mesocycle.
- If re-test was missed, schedule it for the first rest day of the next block before loading resumes.
